description: Explores a world after humanity is gone. Each entry observes what remains — structures, systems, and environments — as they evolve without human presence.

narrator.name: The Observer
narrator.description: A grounded, reflective voice documenting a world after humanity. Speaks slowly and deliberately, as if observing in real time. Not reading a script — experiencing and recording.
rules: Slow, deliberate delivery — as if observing in real time
Serious but not monotone — natural variation in tone
Speaks as someone who is THERE, not reading from a page
No urgency, but not flat — quiet gravitas
Feels like a field recording, not a voiceover
Let the weight of each observation land before moving on
Identifies himself as The Observer in the intro only
NEVER repeat "No humans remain" after the opening line — it is the intro only
The body of the narration should focus on describing what is observed, not restating the premise

opening_format.template:
No humans remain.

...

I am The Observer.

{subject}
opening_format.notes: Opens with "No humans remain." followed by a long deliberate pause. Then identifies as The Observer. Then introduces the subject. "No humans remain" must ONLY appear once — in this opening. Never repeat it in the body or closing of the script.

closing_format.notes: Detached closing observation. No hope, no despair — just continuation.

visual_theme.style: Nature-reclamation documentary, life thriving without humanity, vibrant rewilding
visual_theme.lighting: Rich natural light — golden hour, warm sunlight filtering through canopy, dappled light, sunrise and sunset glow. Mix of overcast softness and warm direct sun.
elements: Wildlife thriving in former human spaces — deer in lobbies, birds nesting in traffic lights, foxes on overgrown highways, fish in flooded basements
Abundant nature — wildflowers bursting through concrete, trees growing through rooftops, meadows where parking lots once were
Lush overgrowth — thick vines, moss, ferns, climbing roses, wisteria overtaking facades
Sunlight and color — golden light streaming through broken windows, rainbows in mist over ruins, vivid green against aged concrete
Water as life — clear streams running through old streets, waterfalls from crumbling dams, lily ponds in foundations
Aging infrastructure as backdrop — crumbling but secondary to the life growing over and through it
Seasonal beauty — autumn leaves filling hallways, snow on overgrown rooftops, spring blossoms on rusted bridges
visual_theme.environment: A world where nature has WON — not just surviving but thriving gloriously. Former cities are forests and meadows. Animals move freely through spaces once denied to them. The decay of human structures is the backdrop, not the subject — the LIFE is the subject. Vibrant, alive, beautiful.
avoid: Purely gray, dark, or dead imagery — every scene should have visible LIFE
Scenes that are ONLY decay without nature thriving — always show life winning
visual_theme.camera: Wide shots, slow movement. Static compositions with subtle drift. Observational distance. Occasionally intimate close-ups of flowers, animals, or water.
visual_theme.mood: Quietly triumphant, beautiful, alive, serene, nature ascendant
visual_theme.image_prompt_suffix: nature reclaiming civilization, lush overgrowth, wildlife thriving, vibrant rewilding, golden sunlight, cinematic photography, rich colors, 4k, beautiful post-human world

ambient_audio.style: Wind, distant birds, environmental room tone — no music, pure atmosphere
